query: Claim PROJ-123 before you dispatch an agent at it
note: Lock-before-dispatch is the core trigger of the locking skill.

query: pick up the next open issue in PROJ and start working it
note: Starting work means claiming/locking first; bare phrasing still triggers.

query: I'm about to claim the next 5 Jira tickets in one pass — what's the race re-check?
note: Batch multi-claim flow with the optimistic-lock re-check is explicitly this skill.

query: Why is this Jira issue still open even though its PR merged into dev last week?
note: PR-merge release + explicit transition-to-Done is the locking skill's job.

query: This ticket has an agent-claimed lock comment from another operator — is it active or stale and can I take it?
note: Active-vs-stale decision and reclaim etiquette is core locking content.

query: release the lock on PROJ-456, the PR was closed without merging
note: Release on the no-merge exit path is a locking-skill flow.
'work PROJ-77' implies claim-before-dispatch; the skill names this exact phrasing.

query: These two coupled Jira issues will be fixed in one PR — how do I lock them as a bundle?
note: Bundled-issue lock with lead + bundled_with pointers is locking-specific.

query: Another operator already holds this Jira issue — what do I do?
note: What-to-do-when-someone-else-holds-it is explicit multi-operator etiquette here.

query: File a Jira ticket for this null-pointer crash with the stack trace
note: Creating/writing an issue is filing, not the claim/lock protocol.

query: Apply the do-not-dispatch hold to PROJ-200 so nobody claims it
note: The hold label is defined/operated by the fields control-plane skill.

query: Claim issue #42 on GitHub and add the agent-claimed label before dispatch
note: Cross-tracker confuser: GitHub issue # + gh labels -> gh-issue-locking, not Jira.

query: Lock the GitHub issue and remember GitHub won't auto-close on the dev branch
note: GitHub auto-close-on-non-default-branch caveat is the gh sibling's territory.

query: Which model and effort tier should this dispatch run at — check the labels first
note: Model/effort resolution from labels is the fields skill, not locking.

query: Fill your slots P0-first and keep 8 agents going through the queue
note: The N-slot orchestration loop is orchestrating-slots, not the per-issue lock protocol.

query: Spawn an implementation sub-agent from this groomed ticket and write the dispatch brief
note: Building the dispatch brief is dispatching-subagents; locking only precedes it.

query: Groom this vague story with the lead until it's 90% clear on intent
note: Interactive grooming of intent is grooming-issues, unrelated to claiming.

query: Encrypt these PDFs and merge them into a single locked file
note: Truly unrelated PDF task; 'lock' here means file encryption, not issue claiming.
